metadata.title: Why Claude's click lands in the wrong spot — and the one ratio that fixes it
metadata.purpose: Multiply the model's coordinates by (original ÷ sent) per axis — the inverse of the resize ratio maps perceived pixels back to physical pixels.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: screen coordinates, image resizing, aspect ratios
metadata.exclusions: DOM navigation, stable element refs, macOS-specific paths
metadata.visual_object: Two aligned screenshots ("Claude sees 1456×819" / "Desktop 1920×1080") with a clickpoint on each and the scaling formula bridging them
metadata.hook: Claude sees a downscaled screenshot but must click the full-size desktop; the coordinates it returns don't line up with the real screen.

beats.narration_text: Screen is 1920×1080; the API sends Claude a 1456×819 view. Claude says "click (700, 410)." Sent raw, that misses. Scale by the inverse ratio → (700·1920/1456, 410·1080/819) = (960, 540) — dead center on the button.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If Claude returns coordinates for a 1456×819 image, how do you translate them to a 1920×1080 screen without the click landing wrong?
Claude sees a downscaled screenshot but must click the full-size desktop; the coordinates it returns don't line up with the real screen.
Multiply the model's coordinates by (original ÷ sent) per axis — the inverse of the resize ratio maps perceived pixels back to physical pixels.
